mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the unemployment threshold shows no statistical edge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns when inflation is below 2.0%, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.01 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 0.4% during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient output growth that overrides rate hike concerns, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in <45% of instances during tightening periods, or the signal loses predictive power when the federal funds rate is rising.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate and a falling unemployment rate creates a soft landing environment where monetary policy successfully cools inflation without triggering recession, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition predicts negative returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0% signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of low-growth quarters over a 10-year sample, or the GDP threshold fails to separate winning and losing months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which support consumer spending and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.015 across all rolling 2-year windows, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with weekly equity returns in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with stagnant wage growth erodes real purchasing power and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >60% of high-inflation periods, or the signal fails to predict direction when inflation expectations are anchored below 2.5%.
